info.title: Tasks
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: If you're using a third-party service for project management, you can create and manage any tasks associated with your CRM records by logging them in HubSpot.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the tasks API to associate tasks with your CRM records. You can assign tasks to specific users in your account, keep track of their status, and organize them using different priorities and types.

post.summary: Read tasks by ID or property
post.description: Retrieves a batch of tasks based on the `inputs`, `properties`, and `propertiesWithHistory` fields. The `properties` field lists properties to return, while the `propertiesWithHistory` field includes properties with their previous values. If any specified properties are missing from the requested objects, they will be ignored.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

get.description: Read an Object identified by `{taskId}`. `{taskId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param.  Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object type

delete.description: Move an Object identified by `{taskId}` to the recycling bin.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to archive.

patch.description: Perform a partial update of an Object identified by `{taskId}`. `{taskId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. Provided property values will be overwritten. Read-only and non-existent properties will be ignored. Properties values can be cleared by passing an empty string.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to update.

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object type

post.summary: Archive a batch of tasks by ID
post.description: Moves a batch of tasks identified by the `id` s specified in the `inputs` to the recycling bin.
Create a batch of tasks using the same rules as when creating an individual task

post.summary: Update tasks by ID or property
post.description: update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.

get.description: Read a page of tasks. Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the `paging.next.after` JSON property of a paged response containing more results

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of objects that can be read by a single request

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Create a task with the given properties and return a copy of the object, including the ID. Documentation and examples for creating standard tasks is provided.
Create or update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.
Search for tasks based on filters, properties, sorting, and query parameters.
